design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate surreal level environments, defeat enemies with guitar weapon, collect power-ups and health items, reach level exit
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (health/worthiness) balanced against enemy density and environmental hazards in time-limited stages
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as Wayne from the film, wielding a guitar as weapon, progressing through absurdist recreations of real-world locations turned into hostile game worlds
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Heavy reliance on licensed IP humor and surreal enemy design (sentient instruments, coffee cups, disco balls) over traditional platformer logic; film references and absurdist tone take precedence over mechanical depth

difficulty.lives_system: Player starts with multiple lives; extra lives awarded through bonus level donut collection and heart pickups
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based checkpointing; bonus stages appear between main levels as risk/reward collection opportunities
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited; fixed level design with predetermined enemy patterns; no difficulty select or adaptive scaling documented

progression.upgrade_system: Guitar power-ups (amps for larger chords, chorus for multiple simultaneous chords, distortion for altered projectile paths, homing projectiles); worthiness/health increases via thumbs-up items and donuts
progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential level progression through four themed worlds (Music Store → Donut Shop → Gasworks → Suburban Fantasy); hidden level select accessible via cheat code (X+L+R, Up+Y+B during opening scene)
replayability_hooks: Hidden level select for non-linear access to stages
Bonus levels with perfect collection targets (all donuts for extra life)
Power-up variety encourages different tactical approaches

world_building_techniques: Surreal environmental design reflecting film locations transformed into hostile game spaces
Absurdist enemy design tied to locations (musical instruments in music store, food items in donut shop, disco elements in nightclub)
Opening premise: Wayne and Garth sucked into arcade game Zantar; rescue mission structure
Character-specific dialogue and cutscenes between levels

boss_design.philosophy: Final confrontation with Zantar (purple gelatinous cube antagonist) framed as climactic rescue; emphasis on thematic enemy design over mechanical complexity
notable_bosses: Zantar - final level antagonist, described as 'purple gelatinous cube' capturing Garth

faction_indicators: Wayne and Garth vs. antagonistic forces (Zantar, captured friend dynamic)
Ben (villain producer) as opposing faction from film adaptation
danger_signals: Sentient objects perverting normal environments (music store instruments become hostile, food becomes dangerous)
Escalating enemy complexity per level (music store → donut shop → nightclub → fantasy suburb)
Garth's capture creating urgency and rescue mission framework
environmental_storytelling: Four distinct themed worlds reflecting real locations transformed into surreal game spaces
Enemy types contextualized to location (accordions/bagpipes in music store, coffee cups/grease in donut shop, disco balls/Yellow Submarines in nightclub)
Progression from recognizable real-world settings toward pure fantasy indicating escalating danger

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Wayne's World (SNES) article
